preamble: R-vocalization into a palatal glide occurs in various dialects and speech stages, reflecting a reduction in the articulatory effort of the rhotic and its transition into a semivowel.

examples.note: In certain rural Brazilian Portuguese dialects, the coda /r/ vocalizes to a palatal glide /j/.
A common phonological substitution in child language development where the complex rhotic is replaced by the simpler palatal glide.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
